features.claim: Antigravity supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to securely connect the editor to local tools, databases, and external services for real-time AI context.
features.name: MCP Integration
features.quote: Antigravity supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard that allows the editor to securely connect to your local tools, databases, and external services. This integration provides the AI with real-time context beyond just the files open in your editor.

features.claim: The AI can read data from connected MCP servers — such as live database schemas or build logs — to inform its code suggestions.
features.name: Context Resources
features.quote: The AI can read data from connected MCP servers to inform its suggestions. When writing a SQL query, Antigravity can inspect your live Neon or Supabase schema to suggest correct table and column names.

features.claim: MCP enables Antigravity to execute specific, safe actions defined by connected servers, such as creating issues or searching documentation.
features.name: Custom Tools
features.quote: MCP enables Antigravity to execute specific, safe actions defined by your connected servers. Example: 'Create a Linear issue for this TODO.' Example: 'Search Notion or GitHub for authentication patterns.'

features.claim: A built-in MCP Store lets users browse, install, and manage supported MCP server integrations directly within the editor.
features.name: MCP Store
features.quote: Connections are managed directly through the built-in MCP Store. Access the Store: Open the MCP Store panel within the '...' dropdown at the top of the editor's side panel. Browse & Install: Select any of the supported servers from the list and click Install.

features.claim: Users can connect arbitrary custom MCP servers by editing a raw JSON configuration file, supporting both stdio and remote HTTP transports.
features.name: Connecting Custom MCP Servers
features.quote: To connect to a custom MCP server: Open the MCP store via the '...' dropdown at the top of the editor's agent panel. Click on 'Manage MCP Servers'... Modify the mcp_config.json with your custom MCP server configuration.

features.claim: Antigravity supports multiple authentication methods for MCP servers including Google Application Default Credentials, OAuth (with automatic token refresh), and custom HTTP headers.
features.name: Authentication
features.quote: Antigravity can automatically handle OAuth for servers that support dynamic client registration (DCR)... Access tokens are stored in ~/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_oauth_tokens.json. Expired tokens are refreshed automatically, and invalid tokens are removed.
